data.description:
## Goal

Overhaul the New Workspace dialog to give power users a richer, more spatially scannable starting point. Recents becomes a first-class panel with rich per-row metadata; blueprints get letter-labeled cells so users can read them at a glance; several power-user affordances thread through (pin/keyboard/drag-drop/empty-state, last-layout memory).

Branch from origin/main @ 5fc161aaa62a8528ca176a0a45741e47e4aa9473.

## Scope

### Recents-as-panel (replaces dropdown)
- Always-visible, vertically scrollable list. ~6 rows in viewport.
- Per-row: project name, full path (mono dim), last opened, open count, pin star.
- Pinned rows lock to top regardless of sort.
- Sort toggle (Most recent / Most opened) in footer.
- Click selects. Double-click or Return opens immediately with last layout.
- Arrow keys navigate when focus not in input.
- Drag a folder from Finder to set base directory.
- Empty state for fresh installs.
- Store cap raised from 8 to 50.
- Data model: RecentDirectory { path, lastOpenedAt, openCount, pinned }. Migrate from existing string-array UserDefaults.

### Base directory block
Merged: directory path + Browse at top, 'or select from your recent directories:' caption, then list.

### Workspace name field
Preserved between recents block and Default layouts. Placeholder syncs with directory's last-path-segment.

### Default layouts row (horizontal scroll)
- Five layouts: Single, Two columns, Three columns, 2x2, **2x3 (new)**.
- Horizontal scroll; 5th item intentionally clipped.
- Each icon: grid of cells with letter labels. First cell A, rest T. Selection only changes outline/background; icon interior stays calm.
- Legend below: A agent / T terminal / B browser / M markdown.

### Custom blueprints row (no letters in v1)
- Horizontal scroller. Outlined topology icons, name, 'N panes' meta.
- '{N} blueprints' count below.
- 'i' help icon opens popover with agent-first framing + 'Reveal blueprints folder' button.

### Single-selection across both rows
Clicking any layout deselects every other blueprint.

### Footer copy
'Launch your default coding agent in the first pane'.

### Last-selected layout memory
One global UserDefaults key. Falls back to Two columns when unset.

## Out of scope
- Type-aware letter labels on custom blueprints.
- Inline filter/search.
- Already-open indicator.
- Git branch/dirty markers.
- Cmd-click for new window.

## Mock
/tmp/c11-new-workspace-mock.html (operator-reviewed).

## Validation plan
- Sheet opens with new layout.
- Click/dblclick/Return on rows behave correctly.
- Pin/unpin holds across sort modes.
- Arrow keys navigate; typing in inputs doesn't steal nav.
- Drag-folder-from-Finder sets directory.
- Empty state visible after clearing UserDefaults.
- 2x3 creates 6 panes (2 cols x 3 rows).
- Letter cells render correctly for defaults.
- Default-layout selection persists across reopens.
- Popover + Reveal-folder works.
data.title: New-workspace sheet redesign: recents-as-panel, letter-cell layout icons, 2x3 layout, pin/keyboard/drag-drop/empty-state, last-layout memory

data.body:
PR: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/201

Build: clean (c11 + c11-logic both compile, zero warnings in changed code).
Tests: 870 c11-logic tests pass (44.7s).
Tagged build: scripts/reload.sh --tag c11-115 launched cleanly.

Functional validation via socket on the tagged DEV.app:
- new-workspace --layout two-by-three -> 6 terminals in 2 cols x 3 rows
- new-workspace --layout three-column -> 3 terminals in 1 row

Full visual sheet UI (recents panel, letter cells, popover, keyboard nav, drag-drop, empty state) is best validated by operator click-through on the tagged build (PID 67424). All check items listed in the PR body.

Follow-up fix pushed: click-and-drag horizontal scroll on the blueprint rows (default + custom). SwiftUI ScrollView only supports trackpad/wheel out of the box on macOS; the new DragScrollView wraps NSScrollView with an NSPanGestureRecognizer so mouse users can grab and pan the row. Build clean, pushed to https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/201

Operator-driven consolidation pushed (commit a88ef9ac6, PR #201):

1. **One row, not two.** Default layouts + custom blueprints merged into a single horizontally-scrollable Layouts section. 1px divider between starters and customs.
2. **Three defaults only:** Single, Two columns, 2 × 2. Three columns, 2 × 3, and 3 × 2 removed (including the three-column.json / two-by-three.json files I added earlier in this branch and the long-standing six-terminal.json).
3. **Bigger cards:** 176 × 168 with 132 × 78 icon, 14pt label, 11pt 3-line descriptions. Descriptions expanded so they're actually useful.
4. **Legend right-aligned at bottom-right** of the layouts section.
5. **Click-drag refit:** the prior NSPanGestureRecognizer was wedging after a scroll-wheel event interrupted its state machine. Swapped to NSEvent.addLocalMonitorForEvents — fires before AppKit dispatches to recognizers, so wheel/trackpad and drag coexist cleanly.
6. **Visible horizontal scrollbar** (autohidesScrollers = false) so the scroll affordance reads explicitly.

Tagged build c11-115 re-launched with the changes. 870 c11-logic tests still pass.

Pushed daec9d11c to PR #201:

1. **2 × 3 added back as a fourth default**, sitting after 2 × 2. Description: 'Six terminal panes in 2 columns, 3 rows. External 27-inch+ monitor suggested.' Surfaces the external-monitor unlock for users still on a laptop screen.
2. **Info-popover fix.** The button click was working; SwiftUI was silently declining to render the popover because arrowEdge: .bottom anchored it above the button where there wasn't quite enough sheet-relative room. Flipped to arrowEdge: .top so it opens below, plus bumped the tap target to 22×22 with a filled info icon for visibility.

Tagged build c11-115 reloaded.

data.reason: Approved + merged by operator; PR #201 squash-merged as a26309f3a

data.body: Merged. PR #201 squash-merged as a26309f3ade1ed6ab53afb84f95b7eacb7532e85 at 2026-05-22T21:42:24Z. Operator approved direct merge ahead of full CI; build/build-ghosttykit/web-typecheck/workflow-guard-tests/remote-daemon-tests all green pre-merge, with compat-tests (macos-15) and build still pending — per operator direction, not blocking.
